perturbation.original_text: 15. Subject to the provision of Clause 16, the Courts having jurisdiction hereunder, shall exclusively be the courts at Mumbai, India. 15.1 This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of India.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. No warranties of any kind are given with respect to the Confidential information and/or any other information disclosed under this Agreement or any use thereof, except as may be otherwise agreed to in writing. Neither party shall be liable to the other hereunder for amounts representing loss of profits, loss of business or indirect, consequential or punitive damages of the other party in connection with the provision or use of Confidential information hereunder except to the extent that such provision or use is caused by and constitutes a breach of this Agreement. 15. Subject to the provision of Clause 16, the Courts having jurisdiction hereunder, shall exclusively be the courts at Mumbai, India. 15.1 This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of India.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the jurisdiction clause (specifying Mumbai courts) to a section dealing with warranties and liability limitations significantly diminishes its prominence. Jurisdiction clauses should be easily identifiable.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Section 23 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872
perturbation.law_citation: Section 23 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872
perturbation.law_explanation: Section 23 deals with lawful consideration and objects. While not directly contravening this section, placing the jurisdiction clause in an unrelated section and obscuring it raises concerns about whether there was free consent to this clause. By obscuring the clause, it becomes more difficult to argue that both parties truly understood and agreed that all disputes will be settled in Mumbai, India. As such, the validity of this agreement and enforcement of this clause can be questioned under Section 23, as the lack of clarity can be argued to make the 'object' of the agreement partially unlawful because of uncertainty surrounding jurisdiction.
perturbation.justification: The structural flaw of moving the jurisdiction clause to a section dealing with warranties and liability, as described in the Perturbation Explanation, creates a legal contradiction. This is because it obscures the clause, making it more difficult to demonstrate free consent and understanding regarding the agreement on jurisdiction, as argued in the Law Explanation. The absence of readily available law snippets doesn't negate the contradiction; the core issue is the obscuring of a critical clause, undermining its enforceability as per the Structural Flaws Legal Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: 19. All obligations respecting the Confidential information already provided hereunder shall survive in perpetuity after the date that the specific Confidential information was first disclosed.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. The Receiving Party shall not copy or reproduce in any way (including without limitation, store in any computer or electronic system) any Confidential information or any documents containing Confidential information without the Disclosing party’s written consent. 19. All obligations respecting the Confidential information already provided hereunder shall survive in perpetuity after the date that the specific Confidential information was first disclosed.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the clause regarding the perpetual survival of confidentiality obligations to a section addressing copying restrictions reduces its prominence. Such a fundamental term regarding the duration of confidentiality should be very clear.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Section 27 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872
perturbation.law_citation: Section 27 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872
perturbation.law_explanation: Section 27 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872, deals with agreements in restraint of trade, which are void. While a confidentiality agreement isn't inherently a restraint of trade, a perpetual confidentiality obligation, especially if ambiguously placed, might be argued as an unreasonable restraint if it unduly prevents the Receiving Party from engaging in future business activities. By placing the clause among copying restrictions, it diminishes the clarity of its temporal scope. This weakens its enforceability by obscuring the potentially problematic 'in perpetuity' term, raising questions about whether it is a reasonable and proportionate restriction, and thereby creating a risk it could be challenged as an unreasonable restraint of trade.
perturbation.justification: The modification, categorized as a 'Structural Flaw - Legal Contradiction', involves relocating a clause regarding the perpetual survival of confidentiality obligations. The Law Explanation posits that placing the clause among copying restrictions diminishes the clarity of its temporal scope and weakens its enforceability. The definition of 'Structural Flaws Legal Contradiction' states that a contradiction arises when a required legal disclosure is placed in an irrelevant or misleading section, making it difficult for parties to locate or interpret. This fits the scenario as the altered placement affects the clarity and prominence of the perpetual confidentiality obligation.
